target.name: JITX
target.founder: Jonathan Bachrach, Duncan Haldane, Austin Buchan, Patrick Li

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
JITX is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jonathan Bachrach, Duncan Haldane, Austin Buchan, and Patrick Li, having raised $12M to date. The valuation universe includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, notably Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given JITX's stage and data constraints, only the stage-progression method is applicable here. Using total raised ($12M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $24M. The peer-median method cannot support triangulation; although seven robotics peers exist in the broader sector universe, none meet the narrow filtering criteria of same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier company characteristics simultaneously. Applying a sector-wide median would introduce unwarranted noise given heterogeneity in robotics company trajectories and capital efficiency.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $20M to $28M, centered at $24M. This range reflects the stage-progression method's output with a ±15% sensitivity band. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-fit peer benchmarks—limiting external validation—and incomplete visibility into JITX's revenue traction, runway, and technical differentiation relative to named competitors. A fuller peer set at identical stage and similar technology focus, alongside revenue data, would materially sharpen this estimate.
